title: Implementing a Node.js Server with Rate Limiting and Caching
statement:
Your task is to create a Node.js server using Express that implements rate limiting and caching. The server should have two endpoints: "/api/users" and "/api/users/:id". The first endpoint should retrieve a list of users and apply rate limiting to prevent excessive requests. The second endpoint should retrieve a specific user by ID and also apply rate limiting. Additionally, implement caching for all endpoints using Redis.

Implement the following requirements:

* Use the "express-rate-limit" package for rate limiting.
* Use the "redis" package for caching.
* Handle errors properly for all endpoints.
* Test the endpoints with the provided test cases.

Note: This challenge requires a good understanding of rate limiting, caching, and error handling in Node.js.
constraints: Must use the "express-rate-limit" package for rate limiting.
Must use the "redis" package for caching.
Must handle errors properly for all endpoints.

endpoints.description: Retrieve a list of users. The response body should contain an array of user objects. The rate limit should be 5 requests per minute per IP address.
Retrieve a specific user by ID. The response body should contain a user object. The rate limit should be 5 requests per minute per IP address.
